conditions.today: 6 AM—Following yesterday's blazing sunshine, an eerie yet optimistic Thursday is dawning in the Tetons thanks to the arrival of thick, moisture-laden clouds. 3 inches of wind-whipped pow resurfaced the upper elevations overnight, and periods of snow and low-elevation liquid precipitation are expected through the day. Crazy terrain features will form thanks to strong west winds that blast in the 15 to 50+ mph range. The high winds will be aided in their creative powder-plastering campaign by mild temperatures that soar close to 30 at the summit and into the mid- to upper 40s in Teton Village. Come live out life on an inclined plane, where the rational move is to go utterly insane. -Lege
conditions.yesterday: 6 AM-Well, well, well. We were certainly due for a snowstorm that overproduced on its promise, and yesterday's wild weather came through in the clutch. 9 inches of wind-driven pow plastered the upper slopes over the past 24 hours, with 3 of those sprinkled on after the lifts closed. The newly remodeled Rendezvous Mountain will meet Wednesday morning under mostly sunny skies that slowly transition to afternoon cloud cover, producing isolated snow showers. High temperatures will tickle the 20-degree mark outside the 10,400-foot Corbet's Cabin Waffle Factory and rise into the mid-30s down in Teton Village. Gusty winds from the west will be spinning the crystals you kick skyward as they crank in the 10-40 mph range. Who cares if it's the middle of the week? Succumb to the undeniable sunsation of getting lost in the glitterball flotation of a frosty face-shot's solar refraction. -Lege
